flashcards.front: What is the function of the camera lens in the image formation process?
flashcards.back: To collect incoming light rays and focus them onto the image sensor, performing a perspective projection from 3D to 2D.

flashcards.front: What does a photosite on an image sensor measure inherently?
flashcards.back: It inherently measures only the intensity (quantity) of light, not its color. It is monochromatic.

flashcards.front: Why does the Bayer filter pattern have twice as many green filters as red or blue?
flashcards.back: Because the human visual system (and many image processing algorithms) is more sensitive to luminance (brightness) information, which is primarily carried by the green channel.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between quantization and sampling?
flashcards.back: Sampling is measuring light intensity at discrete locations (pixels). Quantization is mapping the continuous range of measured intensity values to a discrete set of digital numbers (defined by bit depth).

flashcards.front: Name one key advantage of a CMOS sensor over a CCD sensor.
flashcards.back: Lower power consumption, lower manufacturing cost, faster readout speed, or on-chip integration of other circuitry (e.g., ADC).
